headline: RM12.8 billion defence procurement bypassed open tender
context: Defence Ministry awarded RM12.8 billion in contracts through direct negotiation between 2023-2025, circumventing Treasury guidelines requiring open competitive bidding.

cards.big: RM12.8 billion in defence deals made without competitive bidding
cards.sub: Direct negotiation used for 78% of all military procurement in three years

cards.big: Treasury Instruction 197 allows direct negotiation only for emergencies
cards.sub: Defence Ministry classified routine equipment purchases as 'urgent operational needs'

cards.big: Open tender saves government 15-30% compared to direct negotiation
cards.sub: Potential savings of RM1.9-3.8 billion lost through non-competitive process

cards.big: Defence procurement committee includes no independent civilian oversight
cards.sub: All 7 members are serving military officers or ministry political appointees

cards.big: National security cannot be a blank cheque — secrecy enables both protection and corruption
Should a bipartisan parliamentary committee vet all defence contracts above RM100 million?
